title: Racosperma effusum
taxonomy: Racosperma effusum is actually known as Acacia effusum under the old classification. Acacia effusum is a species of Acacia, a genus of flowering plants in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and is also known as a shrub or small tree. The classification of Racosperma effusum has changed over time, but it is now generally considered to be part of the Acacia genus. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of this species.

intro: Racosperma effusum, also known as the Coast Mimosa or Mimosa hostilis, is a perennial shrub native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Racosperma effusum contains bioactive constituents such as L-DOPA, a precursor to neurotransmitters like dopamine, serotonin, and norepinephrine, as well as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Racosperma effusum include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine and folk remedies for their purported therapeutic effects.
